title: Hands-on Exercise 1
text:
The code chunk below uses p_load() of pacman package to check if tidyverse packages are installed in this computer. If they are,then they will be launched into R.

pacman::p_load(tidyverse)



exam_data <- read_csv("data/Exam_data.csv")



ggplot(data = exam_data, 
       aes(x = fct_infreq(RACE))) + 
  geom_bar()

title: Hands-on Exercise 1
text:
The code chunk below uses p_load() of pacman package to check if tidyverse packages are installed in this computer. If they are,then they will be launched into R.

pacman::p_load(tidyverse)

title: Hands-on Exercise 1
text: exam_data <- read_csv("data/Exam_data.csv")

title: Hands-on Exercise 1
text:
ggplot(data = exam_data, 
       aes(x = fct_infreq(RACE))) + 
  geom_bar()

title: Hands-on Exercise 1
section: R Graphics
text:
R Graphics

exam_data$MATHS <- as.numeric(exam_data$MATHS)
hist(exam_data$MATHS)

title: Hands-on Exercise 1
section: ggplot
text:
ggplot

ggplot(data=exam_data, aes(x = MATHS)) +
  geom_histogram(bins=10, 
                 boundary = 100,
                 color="black", 
                 fill="grey") +
  ggtitle("Distribution of Maths scores")

title: Hands-on Exercise 1
section: Call the ggplot() function
text:
Call the ggplot() function

ggplot(data=exam_data)

title: Hands-on Exercise 1
section: Adds the aesthetic element
text:
Adds the aesthetic element

ggplot(data=exam_data, 
       aes(x= MATHS))

title: Hands-on Exercise 1
section: Plot a bar chart using geom_bar()
text:
Plot a bar chart using geom_bar()


ggplot(data=exam_data, 
       aes(x=RACE)) +
  geom_bar()

title: Hands-on Exercise 1
section: Plot a dot plot using geom_dotplot()
text:
Plot a dot plot using geom_dotplot()


ggplot(data=exam_data, 
       aes(x = MATHS)) +
  geom_dotplot(binwidth=2.5,         
               dotsize = 0.5) +      
  scale_y_continuous(NULL,           
                     breaks = NULL)

title: Hands-on Exercise 1
section: Create a simple histogram using geom_histogram()
text:
Create a simple histogram using geom_histogram()


ggplot(data=exam_data, 
       aes(x = MATHS)) +
  geom_histogram()

title: Hands-on Exercise 1
section: Modifying a geometric object by changing geom()
text:
Modifying a geometric object by changing geom()

ggplot(data=exam_data, 
       aes(x= MATHS)) +
  geom_histogram(bins=20,            
                 color="black",      
                 fill="light blue")

title: Hands-on Exercise 1
section: Modifying a geometric object by changing aes()
text:
Modifying a geometric object by changing aes()

ggplot(data=exam_data, 
       aes(x= MATHS, 
           fill = GENDER)) +
  geom_histogram(bins=20, 
                 color="grey30")

title: Hands-on Exercise 1
section: Plot the distribution of Maths scores using geom-density()
text:
Plot the distribution of Maths scores using geom-density()


ggplot(data=exam_data, 
       aes(x = MATHS)) +
  geom_density()  








ggplot(data=exam_data, 
       aes(x = MATHS, 
           colour = GENDER)) +
  geom_density()

title: Hands-on Exercise 1
section: Plot boxplots using geom_boxplot()
text:
Plot boxplots using geom_boxplot()


ggplot(data=exam_data, 
       aes(y = MATHS,       
           x= GENDER)) +    
  geom_boxplot() 








ggplot(data=exam_data, 
       aes(y = MATHS, 
           x= GENDER)) +
  geom_boxplot(notch=TRUE)

title: Hands-on Exercise 1
section: Plot the distribution of Maths score by gender using geom_violin
text:
Plot the distribution of Maths score by gender using geom_violin


ggplot(data=exam_data, 
       aes(y = MATHS, 
           x= GENDER)) +
  geom_violin()

title: Hands-on Exercise 1
section: Plot scatterplot using geom_point()
text:
Plot scatterplot using geom_point()


exam_data$ENGLISH <- as.numeric(exam_data$ENGLISH)

ggplot(data=exam_data, 
       aes(x= MATHS, 
           y=ENGLISH)) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 100, by = 20))

title: Hands-on Exercise 1
section: Plot the data points on the boxplots by using both geom_boxplot() and geom_point()
text:
Plot the data points on the boxplots by using both geom_boxplot() and geom_point()


ggplot(data=exam_data, 
       aes(y = MATHS, 
           x= GENDER)) +
  geom_boxplot() +                    
  geom_point(position="jitter", 
             size = 0.5)

title: Hands-on Exercise 1
section: Add mean values by using stat_summary()
text:
Add mean values by using stat_summary()


ggplot(data=exam_data, 
       aes(y = MATHS, x= GENDER)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  stat_summary(geom = "point",       
               fun = "mean",         
               colour ="red",        
               size=4)               








ggplot(data=exam_data, 
       aes(y = MATHS, x= GENDER)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  geom_point(stat="summary",        
             fun="mean",           
             colour="red",          
             size=4)

title: Hands-on Exercise 1
section: Adding a best fit curve on a scatterplot
text:
Adding a best fit curve on a scatterplot

ggplot(data=exam_data, 
       aes(x= MATHS, y=ENGLISH)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(size=0.5)








ggplot(data=exam_data, 
       aes(x= MATHS, 
           y=ENGLISH)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method=lm, 
              linewidth=0.5)

title: Hands-on Exercise 1
section: Working with facet_wrap()
text:
Working with facet_wrap()

ggplot(data=exam_data, 
       aes(x= MATHS)) +
  geom_histogram(bins=20) +
    facet_wrap(~ CLASS)

title: Hands-on Exercise 1
section: Plot a trellis plot using facet_grid()
text:
Plot a trellis plot using facet_grid()


ggplot(data=exam_data, 
       aes(x= MATHS)) +
  geom_histogram(bins=20) +
  facet_grid(~ CLASS) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 100, by = 40))

title: Hands-on Exercise 1
section: Flip the horizontal bar chart into vertical bar chart by using coord_flip()
text:
Flip the horizontal bar chart into vertical bar chart by using coord_flip()


ggplot(data=exam_data, 
       aes(x=RACE)) +
  geom_bar() +
  coord_flip()

title: Hands-on Exercise 1
section: Fix both the y-axis and x-axis range from 0-100
text:
Fix both the y-axis and x-axis range from 0-100

ggplot(data=exam_data, 
       aes(x= MATHS, y=ENGLISH)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method=lm, 
              size=0.5) +  
  coord_cartesian(xlim=c(0,100),
                  ylim=c(0,100))

title: Hands-on Exercise 1
section: Plot a horizontal bar chart using theme_gray()
text:
Plot a horizontal bar chart using theme_gray()


ggplot(data=exam_data, 
       aes(x=RACE)) +
  geom_bar() +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_gray()








ggplot(data=exam_data, 
       aes(x=RACE)) +
  geom_bar() +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_classic()








ggplot(data=exam_data, 
       aes(x=RACE)) +
  geom_bar() +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_minimal()

title: ISSS608-VAA
text: Welcome to ISSS608 Visual Analytics and Application homepage. In this website, you will find my coursework prepared for this course.
